food_subgroup: Deep-fried potato snacks / seasoned curly fries

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for seasoned curly fries; closest parent is french fries food product (FOODON_00002694).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to seasoned curly fries; A00LC (Potato products and dishes) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for seasoned curly fries as a specific food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Tera Loopys serving at Potato Corner Singapore is a double-portion of seasoned curly fries, approximately 300 g, customizable with two flavourings.

nutrient_source.title: French Fries, fast food (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 21137); adapted for seasoned curly fries with seasoning sodium adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fast-food french fries used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, fibre, and sugar are taken directly from this entry (per 100 g). Sodium is adjusted upward to approximately 450 mg per 100 g to reflect the heavy seasoning characteristic of Potato Corner flavoured fries, consistent with published analyses of heavily seasoned fast-food potato products. Saturated fat is retained from the USDA source. Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat values are reported as -1 as they are not consistently reported for this product category in the primary source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — French fries, deep-fried (GI 75)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, 2008)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 (high) is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) for deep-fried french fries. Tera Loopys are deep-fried potato curly fries with seasoning; the potato base and deep-frying method are directly analogous. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 123 g carbohydrate in 300 g serving) / 100 = 92.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from heavy seasoning (approximately 450 mg per 100 g; ~1350 mg per Tera serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Tera Loopys due to the very high sodium load from flavoured seasoning, which can acutely elevate blood pressure; a single Tera serving may provide over 55% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75) and very high glycemic load per serving (~92.6) from a large portion of deep-fried starchy potato
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Tera Loopys or consume only a very small portion infrequently, as the high GI and extremely high glycemic load of this double-portion serving will cause rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation, significantly impairing glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (15 g per 100 g), saturated fat (2.3 g per 100 g), high sodium, and ultra-processed deep-fried nature of the product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Tera Loopys due to the combination of high total fat, saturated fat, and sodium in a large ultra-processed deep-fried serving, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk and adverse lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tera Loopys Curly Fries is a branded double-portion seasoned curly fries product sold by Potato Corner, a Philippines-origin fast-food franchise with outlets in Singapore including Funan Mall. The product consists of spiral-cut potato fries that are deep-fried until crispy and then coated with one or two flavoured seasoning powders chosen by the customer from Potato Corner's signature range, which includes flavours such as barbecue, cheese, sour cream, and chilli. The 'Tera' designation refers to a large double-serving size, making it a sharing or indulgence snack. The fries have a crunchy exterior with a soft, starchy interior, and the seasoning imparts a bold, savoury, and often slightly sweet or spicy flavour depending on the chosen combination. Nutritionally, Tera Loopys is a high-energy, high-carbohydrate, high-fat, and high-sodium snack. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 312 kcal, 41 g carbohydrate, 15 g fat, 3.4 g protein, and 450 mg sodium. The glycemic index is high (GI 75), and the glycemic load for the full Tera serving is very high (~92.6), making this product clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is classified as an ultra-processed snack food. In Singapore, it is commonly consumed as a street snack or mall food court treat, particularly popular among younger consumers. Clinically, it should be treated as an occasional indulgence food rather than a regular dietary component.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Tera Loopys?
What is the GI of curly fries?
How much sodium in seasoned curly fries?
What flavours does Potato Corner have?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for Tera Loopys by Potato Corner Singapore exists in any approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA Branded, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for deep-fried fast-food french fries (the closest defensible analogue), with sodium adjusted upward to reflect heavy seasoning. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried french fries. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (fast-food french fries) as no exact branded product data for Potato Corner Tera Loopys is available in any approved source. Sodium has been adjusted upward to ~450 mg/100 g to reflect the heavy flavoured seasoning characteristic of this product. 2. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate for the Tera (double) portion based on typical large fast-food fry serving weights; actual weight may vary. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are set to -1 as these values are not consistently reported for this product category in the primary source (USDA SR Legacy for fast-food fries). 4. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as they are not reported in the adapted source for this product category. 5. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 for deep-fried french fries; no direct GI measurement for Potato Corner Tera Loopys exists in the approved GI literature. 6. Halal status is assumed based on Potato Corner Singapore's general halal certification status; this should be verified against the current MUIS halal certificate. 7. Allergen information is indicative; actual allergens depend on the specific seasoning flavour chosen and may include milk, gluten, or other allergens. 8. The food is classified as Western/Fast Food cuisine for ontology and multilingual purposes; Singaporean multilingual naming rules are applied as this product is sold in Singapore.
